headline: The Funding Gap Between Schools Nobody Publishes
context: Malaysia funds national, Chinese, and Tamil schools under different formulas, but the per-pupil breakdown has never been officially published.

cards.big: Three school systems. The funding comparison is unpublished.
cards.sub: No ministry document shows per-pupil spending across all three streams.

cards.big: SJKC schools raise 30–60% of operating costs from private donations.
cards.sub: The actual cost to parents is far higher than government allocation suggests.

cards.big: Capital allocation for new SJKC and SJKT schools: 14 approved since 2010.
cards.sub: In the same period, 487 SK schools received capital funding.

cards.big: Article 152 protects Malay. Article 12 prohibits education discrimination.
cards.sub: Both are constitutional. The tension is real and unresolved.

cards.big: You cannot debate funding equity without publishing the numbers.
cards.sub: Every side claims disadvantage. Nobody has the data to prove or disprove it.

cards.big: Publish per-pupil spending across all streams. Then the debate can begin with evidence instead of grievance.
